rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ROBERT WHITE ANBIAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 9/24/25. The following issues must be resolved by 9/12/25. (1) The decedent's will does not specify how to distribute real property or the residue of the estate. The petition must demonstrate the authority under which the court can approve the proposed distribution to the surviving siblings only, while excluding B. Bailey, the sole heir of the decedent's predeceased spouse, who died no more than 15 years prior to the decedent. This exclusion is in accordance with Probate Code Section 6402.5, which specifically addresses the decedent's assets that are attributable to the predeceased spouse. (2) Notice of Hearing to B. Bailey and her attorney-in-fact must be given, along with Notice of Directions for Remote Appearances and Access. LRSF. 14.1.C.6). (3) Additionally, the petition requests that the court allow the distribution of the decedent's literary estate to be determined by the University of Virginia. However, it is unclear how the court can grant this request since the Inventory and Appraisal (I&A) has not reported this specific asset (the literary estate). Therefore, the petition needs to either remove this request or provide a Supplemental I&A. Once these issues are addressed, the requests for compensation and reimbursement will be reviewed before the hearing. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF BARBARA MOLLER LYONS
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Appearance required. Issues to include: 1. Petitioner requests extraordinary fees in the amount of $19,025, despite requesting statutory fees in the amount of ~$86K but only providing ~$64K in statutory services. Also, both statutory fees and extraordinary fees were charged at $475-$525/hr, despite Petitioner not providing legal services. 2. Petitioner's creditor's claim filed on 1/10/24 in the amount of $4,987.50 was paid without prior court approval. Petitioner alleges that he did know that prior court approval was required and requests that the court approve the claim. Remote appearances may be made. Please review Local Rule 14.1. The following information pertains to appearances by Zoom: WHEN ENTERING "YOUR NAME" ON ZOOM, BOTH THE FIRST AND LAST NAME, AS WELL THE CASE NUMBER, MUST BE INCLUDED. Meeting ID: 160 225 4765 Password: 514879 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252, and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MARTHA L. GREENE
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 9/24/25. Examiner's Notes emailed to attorney with the following issues: 1) The following have not been filed: a. DE-147 b. DE-147S c. Proof of Publication d. Proposed Order 2) Item 5 is incomplete (re: issue of predeceased child) 3) Item 6 is incorrect, as nephews are listed. The first applicable box must be marked. 4) When second generation or more distant heirs are listed, the deceased ancestors through whom they take (or would have taken) must be named and their relationships to the decedent and dates of deaths need to be stated [Local Rule 14.18C]. Please revise Item 8 of Petition.

When second generation or more distant heirs are listed, the deceased ancestors through whom they take (or would have taken) must be named and their relationships to the decedent and dates of deaths need to be stated [Local Rule 14.18C]. Please revise Item 8 of Petition. Note: Margaret Sheperd, who is listed in Item 8 as being "deceased" must be included.
